perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall each secure 1 <PAGE> in writing a similar non-disclosure agreement from each of its employees prior to any disclosure of PROPRIETARY INFORMATION to that person. IEC shall employ in general the same standard of care as it employs with respect to its own proprietary or confidential information of like kind and character.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall each secure 1 <PAGE> in writing a similar agreement from each of its employees prior to any disclosure of INFORMATION to that person. IEC shall employ in general the same standard of care as it employs with respect to its own proprietary or confidential information of like kind and character.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'non-disclosure' and 'PROPRIETARY' creates an in-text contradiction. The original text requires employees to sign a non-disclosure agreement specifically related to proprietary information. The modified text only requires a generic 'agreement' related to 'information,' broadly construed, which weakens the protection of proprietary information and creates a conflict with the overall intention of the NDA, as it now covers all kinds of information. It further contradicts the objective of the agreement, which is to protect PROPRIETARY INFORMATION.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROGRAM OBJECTIVE The objective of the PROGRAM is to provide a mechanism for the sharing of PROPRIETARY INFORMATION which will enable IEC to evaluate the benefits of the EFL System.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 'non-disclosure agreement' for 'PROPRIETARY INFORMATION'. The changed text broadens this to a generic 'agreement' for any 'information'. This creates a direct conflict with the 'PROGRAM OBJECTIVE' which is explicitly focused on sharing 'PROPRIETARY INFORMATION'. The modified text's vagueness weakens the protection of proprietary information, thereby contradicting the purpose and scope outlined in the 'PROGRAM OBJECTIVE'.

perturbation.original_text: There shall be no obligation with respect to PROPRIETARY INFORMATION which: a) is or becomes publicly available without fault of the receiving Party; b) can be shown by competent written proof to have been in the possession of the receiving Party as of the date of the disclosure; c) is disclosed without restriction to the receiving Party by a third party having the legal right to make such disclosures.
perturbation.changed_text: There shall be no obligation with respect to INFORMATION which: a) is or becomes publicly available without fault of the receiving Party; b) can be shown by competent written proof to have been in the possession of the receiving Party as of the date of the disclosure; c) is disclosed without restriction to the receiving Party by a third party having the legal right to make such disclosures.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'PROPRIETARY INFORMATION' to 'INFORMATION', this omission creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause limits the exceptions to the non-disclosure obligation to information that was already proprietary or became public knowledge. The modified text expands these exceptions to *any* information, which makes the NONDISCLOSURE OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION section practically meaningless, because it is now referring to any kind of information
perturbation.contradicted_text: NONDISCLOSURE OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION Each Party agrees to protect PROPRIETARY INFORMATION of the other Party, not to use proprietary information for any purpose other than stated in the PROGRAM OBJECTIVE and not to disclose PROPRIETARY INFORMATION to any third party. The Parties shall each secure 1 <PAGE> in writing a similar non-disclosure agreement from each of its employees prior to any disclosure of PROPRIETARY INFORMATION to that person. IEC shall employ in general the same standard of care as it employs with respect to its own proprietary or confidential information of like kind and character.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the exceptions to the non-disclosure obligation from 'PROPRIETARY INFORMATION' to 'INFORMATION'. This directly contradicts the 'NONDISCLOSURE OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION' section, which specifically focuses on protecting and not disclosing 'PROPRIETARY INFORMATION'. By expanding the exceptions to include any type of information, the modified text effectively renders the original non-disclosure obligation meaningless, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Following the expiration of the PROGRAM, or a termination of the PROGRAM as provided for above, the rights and obligations respecting NONDISCLOSURE OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION set forth in this Agreement will survive and remain in effect for a period of five years.
perturbation.changed_text: Following the expiration of the PROGRAM, or a termination of the PROGRAM as provided for above, the rights and obligations respecting NONDISCLOSURE set forth in this Agreement will survive and remain in effect for a period of five years.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION', we create an internal contradiction. This clause is supposed to extend the protection of proprietary information beyond the program's term. However, by removing the specific mention of proprietary information, the clause's scope becomes ambiguous. Does it cover all information or only proprietary information? This contradicts the main purpose of the NDA which is to protect PROPRIETARY INFORMATION.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROGRAM OBJECTIVE The objective of the PROGRAM is to provide a mechanism for the sharing of PROPRIETARY INFORMATION which will enable IEC to evaluate the benefits of the EFL System.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifically refers to 'NONDISCLOSURE OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION'. The changed text removes 'OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION', making the scope of nondisclosure ambiguous. The contradicted text, 'PROGRAM OBJECTIVE The objective of the PROGRAM is to provide a mechanism for the sharing of PROPRIETARY INFORMATION,' clearly states that the program is about sharing PROPRIETARY INFORMATION. By removing 'OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION', the changed text creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether the nondisclosure obligation applies only to proprietary information as defined in the Program Objective, or to all information, thus contradicting the program's defined purpose. This fits the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: ASSIGNMENT This Agreement shall not be assigned by either Party without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that either Party may assign its rights under the Agreement to the successors or assigns of all or substantially all of its business assets relating to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: ASSIGNMENT This Agreement shall not be assigned by either Party without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that either Party may assign its rights under the Agreement to the successors or assigns of its business assets.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'all or substantially all of' and 'relating to the subject matter of this Agreement', this creates an in-text contradiction. The original text permits assignment only when the entire business is transferred, ensuring the NDA stays aligned with the original intent. The modified version allows assignment with any business asset transfer. This potentially contradicts the NONDISCLOSURE OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION obligation because a party could assign its rights to a competitor after a small asset transfer
perturbation.contradicted_text: NONDISCLOSURE OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION Each Party agrees to protect PROPRIETARY INFORMATION of the other Party, not to use proprietary information for any purpose other than stated in the PROGRAM OBJECTIVE and not to disclose PROPRIETARY INFORMATION to any third party. The Parties shall each secure 1 <PAGE> in writing a similar non-disclosure agreement from each of its employees prior to any disclosure of PROPRIETARY INFORMATION to that person. IEC shall employ in general the same standard of care as it employs with respect to its own proprietary or confidential information of like kind and character.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts assignment to transfers of 'all or substantially all' business assets related to the agreement's subject matter. The changed text removes this restriction, allowing assignment with any business asset transfer. This contradicts the NONDISCLOSURE OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION obligation, as it enables a party to potentially assign its rights to a competitor after a minor asset transfer, violating the intended protection of proprietary information outlined in the contradicted text. The change directly undermines the original intent of the assignment clause to protect confidential information, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: ENTIRE AGREEMENT This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding of the Parties and may only be modified by an amendment in writing executed by authorized persons on behalf of both IEC and EFL.
perturbation.changed_text: ENTIRE AGREEMENT This Agreement constitutes the understanding of the Parties and may only be modified by an amendment in writing executed by authorized persons on behalf of both IEC and EFL.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'the entire', this creates an in-text contradiction by suggesting the agreement only covers part of the understanding between the parties, not the complete understanding. This can lead to disputes over whether unwritten agreements or understandings also apply, creating ambiguity that directly contradicts the purpose of an 'entire agreement' clause, which is to prevent reliance on prior or contemporaneous agreements not contained in the written contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DEFINITIONS PROGRAM as used herein means an exploratory exchange and evaluation of technical and business information among the Parties pertaining to the EFL System. PROPRIETARY INFORMATION - any information disclosed by one PARTY to the respective other PARTY, which is transferred: (a) by means of documents including, but not limited to, agreements, drawings or pictures, designated as confidential at the time of disclosure and marked accordingly; (b) any specimen, physical item or any other object, whether it be marked "confidential" or not;
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement constitutes 'the entire understanding' while the changed text removes 'the entire', suggesting the agreement may only cover a portion of the understanding. The contradicted text defines 'PROGRAM' and 'PROPRIETARY INFORMATION,' implying these definitions are part of the 'entire understanding' between the parties as previously stated. By removing 'the entire', the modification introduces ambiguity as to whether other unwritten understandings or agreements exist outside the written contract. This contradicts the purpose of the Entire Agreement clause and weakens the definitions, potentially leading to disputes, which is a clear contradiction.
